cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer here. The question is about a story, and the question is: In the story, fitting a working telephone booth __. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall the details from the story. Let me go through the text again to pick up the relevant parts. The story starts with a man from the telephone department getting off a bus and going to a tea stall. There's a mention of the phone line being installed a year ago. Then six months later, men from public works came to put up the phone booth. Then days after that, a painter spent an entire day adding the STD Booth sign with the misspelled word "allowed" as "allowded". So the timeline here is: phone line installed a year ago, then six months later (so 1.5 years ago from now?), the booth was put up. Then the painter did the sign. Now, the man is here again months later. The story also mentions that the telephone man says the minister will come next month to inaugurate it, implying that even though the booth was installed, it's not yet officially operational. Looking at the options: A) was a process that had already taken 12 months B) was in the charge of the headman. C) should have been finished more than a year ago D) was an artistic challenge for a local painter Let's break them down. Option A says the process took 12 months. The phone line was installed a year ago (12 months), then six months later the booth was put up. So that's 1.5 years ago. Then the painter came days after. Now the man is here again months later. The total time from the phone line being installed to the present would be a year plus six months (18 months) plus some months. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth.
